title: Friday Sermon 4 May 2001
description:
Say 'If you love Allah then follow me and Allah will love you and forgive you your faults.  For Allah is Most forgiving, Merciful.'

('The Holy Quran'.  Āl Imran [Family of Imran]: 32).

Allah the Exalted unfolds the way to His love in a unique manner in 'The Holy Quran'.  Believers are enjoined to follow and love the Messenger of Allah (peace and blessings of Allah be on him). Divine grace descends through this and forgiveness is granted. Allah, the angels and mankind curse people who believe and then turn away.

However despite such unequivocal pronouncements, the door to Divine mercy is still open for them on the condition that they amend themselves. In the recurrent Quranic reference of 'whomsoever He pleases' Allah the Exalted forgives when He deems a person's repentance is worthy of acceptance, for He knows the state of one's heart.

'The Holy Quran' proclaims what is forbidden to believers and in light of the fact that some of these severe sins were committed by earlier Arabs, Allah's Mercy is declared. People are reminded of Allah's continual Mercy in matters of dishonesty in trade, which is compared to killing, the connotation is to be merciful and kind to people just as Allah is Merciful to them.*  

Obedience of the Messengers of Allah is conditional to Allah's pleasure, through which the blessing of intercession can be achieved.

Allah the Exalted bestows special grace on those who strive in His way and if they repent, those who miss out are forgiven. Allah is Most Forgiving to those who truly emigrate in His cause and promises them a great reward.**

Mankind is enjoined never to lose hope of Allah's Mercy. If forgiveness is sought with the sincerity of heart, Allah the Exalted is Merciful always. Those who do not make any distinction between Allah’s prophets can hope for His reward.***

The Holy Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be on him) said that unless Allah's Mercy covers us, our deeds alone couldn't be the means of salvation.

The Promised Messiah (on whom be peace) wrote that: Through Divine Mercy the endeavors of those who strive in the way of Allah bear fruit. In the Quranic terminology, Allah is called the Merciful when He accepts supplication, deep humility and the good deeds of people and protects them from misfortune and calamities.

Mercy necessitates entreating and for that good deeds are essential. 'The Holy Quran' states that a person's supplications are exalted by their good deeds.

* Al Nisa [The Women]: 30.  
** Ibid, 101.
*** Ibid, 153.
